reasoning: The 6-month Treasury yield has shown a clear downward macro trend over the last year alongside expected Fed rate cuts, but the most recent value of 3.76% (as of 2025-10-24) represents a local minimum relative to the recent month's average (near 3.80%). Short-term statistical modeling via FRED indicates a high likelihood of minor mean-reversion with a linear predicted value of 3.78% by early November, suggesting a short-term increase. As the horizon extends, the broader macro trend of Federal Reserve easing weighs down on the yield curve, conflicting with the short-term mean reversion. Per instructions, uncertainty increases over time, anchoring longer-horizon estimates closer to 0.5. Therefore, the highest probability of increase is in the short-term (1-week horizon), slowly declining toward ~0.50 in the 3-month horizon.
